untranslatable_reason: This signal is a position sizing/leverage rule, not a directional trading entry/exit signal. It requires: (1) calculation of half-Kelly leverage from strategy win rate and risk/reward ratio (not available as indicators), (2) computation of maximum historical one-period loss from backtest statistics (requires meta-analysis of returns distribution, not point-in-time indicator values), and (3) dynamic leverage adjustment based on drawdown thresholds (requires portfolio equity tracking and historical drawdown analysis). The backtest engine rule structure is designed for price/indicator conditions, not for meta-parameters and portfolio-level statistics. This requires a separate position sizing module outside the standard indicator-based rule framework.
translation_notes: CHAN_QT_DRY_0 is a risk management overlay (Kelly criterion + historical drawdown analysis) rather than a tradeable signal. Implement this as a separate position sizing algorithm that: (1) calculates half-Kelly from win_rate and payoff_ratio, (2) derives max_leverage = max_acceptable_drawdown / max_historical_one_period_loss, (3) applies leverage = min(half_kelly, max_leverage), and (4) rebalances weekly/daily/hourly. Exit condition should trigger when realized drawdown exceeds max_acceptable_drawdown threshold.
